label: Uneasy calm
summary: The world stayed pinned just below neutral, its uneasy-calm band tested by a fresh flashpoint at sea even as diplomacy in Ankara delivered. Overnight on July 7 Iran's Revolutionary Guard fired missiles at commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, striking the Qatari LNG carrier Al-Rekayyat and damaging a Saudi-flagged crude tanker, sending Brent up about 3 percent to roughly 74 dollars a barrel and rattling a US market already unnerved by a semiconductor sell-off; the Dow slipped from its record to close near 52,925 as chipmakers sank more than 4 percent. The attack reopened questions about the fragile February-war truce Washington and Tehran are still trying to make permanent. Weather and humanitarian ledgers stayed heavy: Super Typhoon Bavi, packing sustained winds near 250 kilometres per hour, tracked toward Taiwan for Friday and Saturday before an expected run into eastern China near Shanghai, with almost 29,000 Taiwanese troops on standby and forecasts of more than a metre of rain; Venezuela's twin-quake toll held at 3,535 dead with nearly 18,000 homeless; the RSF's siege of Sudan's El Obeid left half a million trapped in darkness; and DR Congo's Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak passed 506 deaths. Against that ran real lift. At the NATO summit's second day in Ankara, allies moved to pledge some 70 billion euros a year in military aid to Ukraine for 2026 and 2027, and Presidents Trump and Zelenskyy were set to meet, with Zelenskyy citing 'a real prospect of ending this war.' Australia and Fiji sealed a historic 'Ocean of Peace' mutual-defence pact, and a first clinical trial of Ebola therapies offered guarded hope. The headline settles at 42, a notch below neutral, weighed by the Hormuz strike, storm and siege even as summitry and Pacific diplomacy quietly delivered.

drivers.headline: Iran's Revolutionary Guard fires at least two missiles at commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz overnight, striking the Qatari liquefied-natural-gas carrier Al-Rekayyat about eight nautical miles off Oman and setting off an engine-room fire, and damaging a Saudi-flagged crude oil tanker, with no casualties reported but the attack reopening fears over the fragile interim peace between Washington and Tehran after their February 2026 war, as shipping through the strait remains far below the pre-war level of 120 to 140 daily crossings

Oil prices jump after the Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks, with Brent crude futures settling about 3 percent higher at 74.16 dollars a barrel and US West Texas Intermediate advancing 2.8 percent to 70.44 dollars, as the United States revokes an Iran sale authorization and traders weigh renewed risk to the waterway that carries roughly a fifth of the world's seaborne oil

US stocks pull back from record highs as a semiconductor sell-off deepens, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average shedding 130.76 points, or 0.25 percent, to close at 52,925.15, the Nasdaq Composite falling 1.16 percent and the S&P 500 slipping 0.45 percent, after Samsung's quarterly results and reports that China's DeepSeek is developing its own AI chip rattled the sector and a gauge of chipmakers sank more than 4.5 percent, with Micron down 4.7 percent

Super Typhoon Bavi, the year's strongest storm with maximum sustained winds near 250 kilometres per hour and an expansive storm-force wind field roughly 926 kilometres across, tracks toward Taiwan for Friday and Saturday before an expected run into eastern China near Shanghai as a weakening storm, with Taiwan's cabinet warning of more than a metre of rain in places and almost 29,000 military personnel placed on standby for relief efforts

Venezuela's twin-earthquake death toll holds at 3,535 with 16,740 injured and 17,854 left homeless two weeks after back-to-back magnitude-7.2 and 7.5 quakes struck within 39 seconds of each other west of Caracas, as rescue efforts wind down and Venezuelans face the grim task of recovering their own dead, with more than 30,000 people reported missing and about 12,800 sheltering across some 80 sites in Caracas and La Guaira

The Rapid Support Forces maintain their siege of El Obeid, capital of Sudan's North Kordofan state, leaving roughly half a million civilians, including some 105,000 already displaced, trapped in a city without electricity for nearly a month as the UN documents at least 15 drone strikes killing more than 45 civilians and damaging hospitals, schools and fuel depots, with food prices up as much as 300 percent and rights groups warning of an El-Fasher-style atrocity

The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo passes 506 confirmed deaths among 1,561 confirmed cases as of early July, with Ituri province worst hit at 1,417 cases and 424 deaths and spread now reaching North Kivu and South Kivu, even as health workers threaten to strike over unpaid benefits, inadequate supplies and insecurity from clashes with armed militias

Israeli air attacks continue to kill Palestinians in Gaza despite the US-brokered October ceasefire, with recent strikes killing at least six people and Gaza's health ministry reporting that truce violations have now killed at least 1,072 and wounded 3,463, while the promised second phase covering Israeli withdrawal and Hamas disarmament remains stalled and no timeline has been set to hand governance to a Palestinian technocratic committee

At the NATO summit's second day in Ankara, alliance leaders move to affirm an iron-clad commitment to collective defence and to pledge some 70 billion euros in military assistance to Ukraine annually for 2026 and 2027, a total of about 140 billion euros over two years, as US President Donald Trump and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy prepare to meet on the sidelines at 14:30 local time, with Zelenskyy citing 'a real prospect of ending this war'

Australia and Fiji sign a historic 'Ocean of Peace' mutual-defence pact and a 'Vuvale Union' economic treaty in Suva, giving Fiji its first mutual-defence treaty and Australia its fourth regional ally, with Prime Minister Anthony Albanese pledging more than one billion Australian dollars in investment over a decade and calling the mutual defence obligation the highest there is, in a diplomatic win seen as countering China's influence in the Pacific

Hope arrives for the Ebola strain ravaging DR Congo as a newly launched clinical trial in Ituri province tests the monoclonal antibody MBP134 and the antiviral remdesivir against Bundibugyo virus disease, for which no treatment had been specifically designed, offering the first prospect of a proven therapy against an outbreak that has killed more than 500 people

Conservation wins pile up midway through 2026 as a record 30,000 endangered Central California Coast coho salmon return to the Mendocino coast over two years, double the previous record, while a global study finds mangrove gains have begun to outpace losses over the past 16 years thanks to restoration and better management, and North America's largest wildlife crossing over Colorado's I-25 is projected to cut wildlife-vehicle collisions by up to 90 percent

sources_consulted: Wikipedia (Typhoon Bavi 2026 / 2026 Venezuela earthquakes / 2026 Ebola epidemic / 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis / Gaza peace plan / Siege of El Obeid / 2026 NATO Ankara summit)
